items.title: The Recovery of the Liberal Arts in Canada
items.summary: Argues that Canadian Catholic universities have largely abandoned the liberal arts tradition that once distinguished them, and calls for a return to formation-centered education rooted in the Great Books.
items.ggiConnection: Directly relevant to GGI mission. Echoes themes from Ryan's 'Case for Catholic Education.'

items.title: Why Leisure Is Not Laziness
items.summary: A fresh reading of Josef Pieper's argument that genuine leisure — contemplation, wonder, festivity — is the basis of culture, not its opposite. Challenges the productivity gospel head-on.
items.ggiConnection: Conference theme connection. Pieper's 'Leisure: The Basis of Culture' is the 2026 Conference text.

items.title: Parish Closures and What Comes After
items.summary: Covers diocese restructuring in Alberta and asks what new models of parish life might emerge. Interviews two pastors navigating mergers with hope rather than nostalgia.
items.ggiConnection: Canadian content. Relevant to the 'building what comes next' narrative.

items.title: Newman's Idea of a University, Reconsidered
items.summary: A philosopher at Notre Dame revisits Newman's vision of liberal education and argues it offers a more radical critique of the modern university than most Catholic educators realize.
items.ggiConnection: Connects to Ryan's latest book on Newman and evangelizing the 'Nones.'

items.title: Are Catholic Schools Still Catholic?
items.summary: A survey of Catholic school administrators reveals a growing gap between institutional identity and classroom practice. Data-heavy and honest about the challenges.
items.ggiConnection: Directly relevant to Ryan's work on Catholic education.

items.title: Recovering the Common Good in Canadian Public Life
items.summary: Argues that Catholic social thought offers Canada's best framework for moving beyond polarization. Cites both papal encyclicals and Canadian policy history.
items.ggiConnection: Cardus / Andrew Bennett connection. Advisory board overlap.

items.title: The Classical Education Movement Is Winning
items.summary: Reports on the rapid growth of classical Catholic schools across North America and profiles three schools that opened in the last two years — including one in Alberta.
items.ggiConnection: Campion Academy connection. Topping family is a founding family.

items.title: Gregorian Chant Is Having a Moment
items.summary: Documents the quiet revival of Gregorian chant in parishes across the English-speaking world, with data on schola formation and young adult participation.
items.ggiConnection: Music Camp connection. GGI crest includes the 'Book of Chant.'

items.title: After Christendom: Building Faith Communities in a Post-Christian World
items.summary: Dreher's follow-up to 'The Benedict Option' shifts from diagnosis to prescription, arguing that small, intentional communities of formation — not culture-war politics — are the path forward.
items.ggiConnection: Directly relevant to GGI's 'islands of holiness' language and community-building mission.

items.title: Review: 'Thinking as Though God Exists' by Ryan Topping
items.summary: A favorable review of Ryan's latest book, praising its synthesis of Newman's thought with contemporary evangelization challenges. Calls it 'the most important Canadian Catholic book of the decade.'
items.ggiConnection: Ryan's own book. Major review — should be shared.

items.title: What Newman Knew About the Nones
items.source: Bishop Robert Barron — Word on Fire (YouTube)
items.summary: 22-minute reflection on Newman's approach to reaching the religiously unaffiliated. Barron cites Ryan Topping's work explicitly at the 14-minute mark.
items.ggiConnection: Barron cites Topping directly. High visibility — Word on Fire audience.

items.title: Why Beauty Evangelizes Better Than Arguments
items.summary: 14-minute video arguing that the Church's most effective evangelization tool has always been beauty — architecture, music, liturgy — not apologetics. Shot in a gorgeous medieval church.
items.ggiConnection: Board member content. Aligns with GGI's beauty-as-formation thesis.

items.title: Aquinas on the Virtues: A Masterclass
items.summary: 45-minute lecture on Aquinas's virtue ethics, accessible to non-specialists. Strong production quality, well-structured.
items.ggiConnection: Relevant to GGI's 'Virtuous Leadership' masterclass offering.

items.title: Episode 412: The Future of Classical Catholic Education
items.summary: Matt Fradd interviews the founder of a new classical Catholic school in Western Canada. Discusses curriculum design, parent formation, and the relationship between liturgy and learning.
items.ggiConnection: Western Canadian content. Classical education is core to GGI's vision.

items.title: Notre-Dame de Montréal: Side Chapel Restoration Complete
items.source: Sacred Architecture Journal / Diocese of Montréal
items.summary: Stunning photography of the newly restored Sacré-Cœur chapel. Gold leaf, carved wood, and painted vaulting returned to their original 1888 glory. A reminder that beauty can be recovered, not just mourned.
items.ggiConnection: Canadian beauty. 'Authentic culture as fruit of revealed worship' — GGI crest.

items.title: Heavenly Bodies: Medieval Manuscript Illumination
items.summary: New exhibition showcasing 40 illuminated manuscripts from the 9th–15th centuries. Includes several pages from Gregory the Great's own 'Moralia in Job.' Free virtual tour available.
items.ggiConnection: Gregory the Great connection. Patron saint — his actual manuscripts on display.
